Caption:
Cooler mornings. Quieter walks. Good company. 💚

There’s something special about the first proper autumn morning with your dog — crisp air, a slower pace, and that calm start to the day before everything gets busy.

Simple moments. Solid company. That’s the good stuff.
Visual_Brief: Hero dog should be an Africanis or South African mixed breed. Natural, real SA setting: early morning suburban street, stoep exit moment, or quiet park path. Dog with owner, walking side-by-side or pausing together. Tone should feel calm, grounded, warm, everyday. No product in frame. No dramatic leaf-fall autumn look — this should feel like early SA autumn. Light jacket or hoodie on owner is fine if subtle. Warm but soft natural morning light. Authentic over polished.

Caption:
From the morning patrol to the last lap around the garden, some dogs are always on the move. ⚡🐾

JOCK Multistage is made for active legends — with quality protein, vitamins and minerals to help support strength, condition, and everyday energy.

For the dogs who don’t really do ‘off days’.
Visual_Brief: Active adult dog, not a puppy. Best breed direction: German Shepherd, Malinois, Border Collie, or Australian Cattle Dog if we want a stronger visual link into APR-003 later. Natural SA outdoor setting: veld edge, farm road, open garden, or trail-like path. Dog should feel athletic, alert, capable. No heavy product-bag hero shot. If bag appears, keep it secondary and natural. Warm, clean light. Framing should feel energetic but still premium.

Caption:
Those ears. That stare. That chaos level. 😅

If your dog had to be matched to a weekend mood, what would they be:
- ready for adventure
- causing mild destruction
- pretending they’ve never heard a command in their life
- asleep before lunch

Tell us your dog’s current mood in the comments. 👇
Visual_Brief: Hero dog: Australian Cattle Dog with strong multi-colour coat detail. Expression should be intense, smart, slightly chaotic, highly observant. Real SA outdoor setting: yard, garden, dusty path, or farm-style edge. Framing should feel character-led, not polished or product-led. No product bag in frame. Dog should be facing camera or strongly engaging viewer. This should feel interactive and comment-friendly. Slight humour in expression is ideal.

Caption:
A quick Easter reminder for dog parents: chocolate and dogs are not a good mix. 🍫🐾

With treats and family snacks around over the long weekend, it’s worth keeping chocolate safely out of reach.

Swipe through for a simple reminder on what to avoid and what to do if your dog gets hold of something they shouldn’t.
Visual_Brief: Carousel format, clear and shareable. Friendly educational tone, not fear-based. Breed direction: Staffy, Jack Russell, or mixed breed. Real home setting with Easter cues kept subtle. No exaggerated chocolate overload visual. Focus on clarity, clean layout, and useful information. Dog should look curious or interested, not distressed. Product not needed in frame. Suggested slide flow: Slide 1 Easter chocolate warning headline; Slide 2 why chocolate is a problem for dogs; Slide 3 common Easter foods to keep away; Slide 4 what to do if your dog eats some; Slide 5 safe reminder to keep it out of reach.

Caption:
School holidays mean one thing for dogs: everyone’s home and the routine is officially gone. 😅🐾

Later breakfasts, more people around, extra door traffic, and at least one child treating the dog like a full-time best friend.

For some dogs, that’s the dream.
For others… it’s a lot.

What changes most for your dog during the school holidays? 👇
Visual_Brief: Lifestyle, relatable, light. Breed direction: Dachshund, Beagle, Staffy, or mixed breed. Dog in a real SA family-home environment. School holiday energy should feel present but natural: kids at home, movement in the background, lived-in family setting. Dog can look either delighted or mildly overstimulated. No product in frame. Warm, authentic, everyday energy. Instagram-friendly composition.

Caption:
The second they hear the packet… full attention 👀🐾

Happy Happies Biltong flavour is the kind of reward that turns “maybe later” into “watch me do it now”.

Perfect for training moments, good behaviour, and those little wins that deserve a proper treat.

What trick would your dog do for a Biltong Happy Happie? 👇
Visual_Brief: Instagram-first, clean and punchy. Dog locked onto owner holding a Happy Happies Biltong treat. Dog should look alert, eager, well-behaved, and excited. Best breed options: Staffy, Jack Russell, Border Collie, Dachshund, or Africanis/mixed breed. Avoid default Labrador/Golden unless there is a strong reason. Natural SA home or garden setting. Warm light, eye-level framing. Treat visible in hand. Pack can appear subtly in frame, but not dominate. Expression should sell the moment: I am 100% focused on that snack.

Caption:
Nothing complicated. Just a dog having a great day. 💚🐾

This is your Feel-Good Friday reminder that sometimes the best part of the week is a wagging tail, a bit of sunshine, and a dog that’s fully living in the moment.

Drop a photo or gif of your dog looking ridiculously happy. 👇
Visual_Brief: Feel-good, bright, uplifting. Hero dog should be Africanis, mixed breed, or another less overused breed with a joyful expression. Real SA setting: sunny garden, community park, or backyard play moment. No autumn leaf-fall cues. Dog should feel genuinely happy: mid-run, smiling at camera, or caught in a playful moment. No product in frame. Facebook-friendly, shareable, comment-friendly. Energy should feel simple, human, warm.

Caption:
Some of the best moments with your dog happen in motion. 💚🐾

The walk before the day starts. The game that goes on longer than planned. The look they give you when they know it’s time to move.

Healthy dogs, active lives, proper companionship — that’s the good stuff.

What’s your dog’s favourite part of the day? 👇
Visual_Brief: Pure lifestyle post, but active and upbeat. Breed direction: German Shepherd, Staffy, Australian Cattle Dog, or mixed breed. Avoid Boerboel and Ridgeback here. Real SA setting: garden play, walk start, dog moving beside owner, or open yard/park energy. Dog should feel healthy, switched on, connected to owner. No product in frame. Natural energy, warm light, real-life feel. The emotional angle should come from shared activity, not stillness.

Caption:
Getting older doesn’t mean slowing down completely — it just means supporting your legend a little differently. 💚🐾

As dogs age, their needs change. The right nutrition can help support condition, mobility, and everyday wellbeing so they can keep enjoying the life they love.

Swipe through for a few signs your older dog may need a little extra support.
Visual_Brief: Educational carousel. Hero dog should be an older dog with visible age, but still bright and dignified. Breed direction: senior mixed breed, older German Shepherd, older Staffy, or older Labrador only if needed but not first choice. Avoid over-polished perfect senior look. Real SA home or garden setting. Tone should feel supportive and positive, not fragile or sad. Dog should still feel present, alert, and cared for. Product can appear lightly in later slide if needed, but not necessary on cover. Suggested slide flow: Slide 1 Older dogs need different support; Slide 2 changes in movement or stiffness; Slide 3 energy and recovery changes; Slide 4 maintaining condition and strength; Slide 5 nutrition support reminder.

Caption:
The good stuff goes into JOCK Multistage — and there’s a reason for that. 💚🐾

Active dogs need food that does more than just fill the bowl. They need balanced nutrition that helps support strength, condition, and the kind of everyday energy that keeps them moving.

That’s why JOCK Multistage is made with quality ingredients that work hard behind the scenes — so your dog can keep showing up like the legend they are.
Visual_Brief: Single post, not carousel. The good stuff should be the clear creative hook. Product bag can be visible here, but not oversized or overly salesy. Visually balance Multistage pack, quality ingredient cues, and a healthy active adult dog. Breed direction: German Shepherd, Malinois, Border Collie, or active mixed breed. Dog should feel strong, healthy, alert. SA outdoor setting preferred. Tone should feel credible, wholesome, and active. Ingredient cues should feel natural and premium, not clinical.

Caption:
Here’s to the dogs who are part of everyday life in South Africa. 🇿🇦🐾

Full of character. Full of energy. Full of heart.

Happy Freedom Day. 💚
Visual_Brief: Freedom Day should feel local, simple, and proudly South African. Hero dog: Africanis or mixed breed. Real SA community setting: township street, community yard, local field, or neighbourhood space. No leash or walk visual. Dog should feel healthy, confident, present, and at home in the space. No touristy SA clichés. No product in frame. Keep it clean, honest, and representative.

Caption:
Tag the friend whose dog is absolute chaos… but somehow still the favourite. 😅🐾

You know the one:
- steals the spotlight
- ignores all personal space
- arrives like a full event
- gets away with everything

Tag them below. 👇
Visual_Brief: Comment-driving community post. Inclusive dog mix in frame: Africanis or mixed breed, small breed, medium breed, and one stronger character dog if needed. Real SA setting with social energy. Should feel playful and slightly chaotic in a relatable way. No product in frame. Expression and composition should support the that one dog joke. Facebook-friendly and highly taggable.

Caption: POV: you opened the food bin 0.4 seconds ago and now you’re being supervised by management. 👀🐾
Visual_Brief: Dog very close to camera with an intense stare. Kitchen or feeding area. Feels like the dog appeared out of nowhere the second the food bin opened. Expression is everything. Best breed options: Africanis, Staffy, Dachshund, or Jack Russell. No product bag needed.

Caption: Big body. Puppy brain. No spatial awareness. No regrets. 😅
Visual_Brief: Giant teenage dog trying to sit on a lap, couch, or another too-small space. Must feel oversized and clueless in a lovable way. Real home setting. Best breed options: Rottweiler teen, Great Dane, Boerboel youngster, or Ridgeback youngster. If product is used, keep it in caption only.

Caption: Can’t hear ‘come here’… but hears a packet open from another province. 👂🐾
Visual_Brief: Dog frozen mid-moment with ears up and a hyper-alert expression. Natural home or garden setting. Quick, simple joke. Best breed options: Beagle, Border Collie, Staffy, Africanis, or mixed breed. Treats angle can be implied visually but keep it clean and not overly product-heavy.
